content.text: @"/home/trido/.claude/uploads/840a1a19-944e-438b-a87c-1fa392ececcc/7630d206-MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md" bạn sẽ là mike của tôi. bạn là manager tổng giúp tôi thực hiện plan này. Mục tiêu của tôi là : "tôi muốn tạo một agent (session) tổng quản lý giúp tôi quản lý tất cả các sessions con tạo ra thông qua agent tổng. Tất cả các agent này đều tạo ra theo dạng remote control. Tôi có một server chạy claudecode rồi. Tôi sẽ tạo các agent dưới dạng remote control trên server, để tôi có thể sử dụng qua claudecode mobile, claudecode desktop mà không bị gián đoạn bất cứ lúc nào. Tất cả các tiến trình sẽ chạy trên server không bị phụ thuộc vào device. Agent tổng tên là Mike. Mọi việc tôi sẽ giao cho agent tổng như tạo ra những agent con. Tổng hợp kết quả của agent con theo giờ. Mục tiêu là làm sao agent tổng qua 30 phút sẽ nắm được hết tất cả công việc mà các agent con đang làm. Đồng thời, các agent con, cũng hiểu được các kết quả đã được cập nhật từ các agent khác mà không cần phải nhắc đi, nhắc lại hoặc theo kiểu không hề biết gì. Agent tổng sẽ phải định kỳ cập nhật kiến thức chung cho tất cả các agent con, cũng như nhắc agent con phải cập nhật kiến thức lên knowledge database mà agent tổng đang quản lý. Agent tổng có thể đại diện trả lời, hoặc điều phối câu hỏi đến các agent con trả lời, sau đó đưa kết quả trả ra cho user. Agent tổng có thể hiểu là đầu mối, điều phối thông tin cho toàn bộ hệ thống agent ở dưới. User có thể làm việc trực tiêp với từng agent con nếu muốn, hoặc thông qua agent tổng". Bạn xem lại plan xem có hợp lý chưa? có cần góp ý thêm gì không?

file.content:
# MIKE — Hệ thống điều phối multi-agent Claude Code trên server

> Bản kế hoạch chi tiết. Mọi năng lực Claude Code dưới đây đã được **xác minh** (workflow nghiên cứu + reality-check, [REDACTED]21) — phần "Sự thật nền tảng" ghi rõ cái gì THẬT, cái gì là NGỘ NHẬN đã bị bác bỏ.
> Agent tổng = **Mike**. Server = **kaffa_v2** (`trido@192.168.100.7`, Linux, Proxmox host `sgms`). Home dự kiến: `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike`.

---

## 0. TL;DR — 6 quyết định cốt lõi

| Yêu cầu của bạn | Cơ chế THẬT dùng để hiện thực | Vì sao |
|---|---|---|
| Agent chạy trên server, điều khiển từ mobile/desktop, không gián đoạn khi rớt thiết bị | Mỗi agent = 1 tiến trình `claude` trong **tmux/systemd** trên server + chạy **`claude remote-control`** (server mode) trên chính nó | Remote Control chỉ gọi HTTPS *ra ngoài*, app chỉ là "cửa sổ". Vì "máy local" chính là server luôn-chạy → điện thoại rớt mạng tùy ý, server vẫn chạy. **Đây là cách THẬT duy nhất để điện thoại chạm vào tiến trình trên server của bạn.** |
| Mike điều phối / giao việc cho agent con | **Message-bus bằng file + BigQuery** (Mike ghi directive, con poll). Lớp tiện lợi *tùy chọn*: Agent Teams (experimental) | `send_message` giữa-phiên **luôn hỏi xác nhận** và **không dùng được ở chế độ tự động** → không thể là lõi điều phối. Bus file/BQ bền, độc lập thiết bị. |
| Con hiểu kết quả của con khác mà không phải nhắc lại | **Context injection**: CLAUDE.md import `context_pack.md` + hook `SessionStart` kéo bản mới + hook `UserPromptSubmit` chèn "delta" khi version đổi | Hook do *harness* chạy (không phải model) → tự động làm tươi mỗi lượt, không cần Mike đẩy thủ công cho từng con. |
| Cứ 30′ Mike nắm toàn bộ việc các con đang làm | **OS cron/systemd-timer** chạy `claude -p` (headless) mỗi 30′ → đọc bus/registry, MERGE BigQuery, sinh digest | OS cron là cơ chế DUY NHẤT sống qua reboot, đúng nhịp 30′. `/loop` chỉ sống trong phiên (tự xóa sau 7 ngày). **Cloud Routine KHÔNG làm được 30′** (tối thiểu 1 giờ). |
| Mike định kỳ cập nhật kiến thức chung + nhắc con ghi KB | Consolidator (cron) rebuild `context_pack.md` + bump version (con tự kéo). **Stop hook** ở mỗi con tự ghi heartbeat + nhắc "đã ghi tri thức chưa?" | Lời nhắc thành hook *xác định*, không phải Mike đi cằn nhằn từng con. |
| User nói chuyện trực tiếp với từng con | Mỗi con có **Remote Control session riêng có tên** (`--name`), hiện trong claude.ai/code & app; hoặc dashboard `claude agents` | Remote Control server-mode đỡ nhiều phiên đặt tên đồng thời (`--capacity` mặc định 32, `--spawn=worktree`). |

**Một câu:** Remote Control = device-independence + nói-chuyện-trực-tiếp · tmux/systemd = độ bền · OS cron + `claude -p` = đồng hồ 30′ · file/BigQuery "append-then-consolidate" = bộ não chung · Agent Teams = lớp nhắn tin live tùy chọn.

---

## 1. Mục tiêu & ràng buộc

**Mục tiêu (theo yêu cầu):**
1. Một agent tổng "Mike" tạo & quản lý mọi agent con.
2. Tất cả chạy trên server dạng remote-control, dùng được qua Claude Code mobile/desktop, không gián đoạn, không phụ thuộc thiết bị.
3. Cứ 30 phút Mike nắm trọn việc của mọi con.
4. Con hiểu kết quả mới của các con khác, không phải nhắc lại.
5. Mike định kỳ phát kiến thức chung + nhắc con ghi lên knowledge DB.
6. Mike có thể tự trả lời, hoặc định tuyến câu hỏi xuống con rồi trả kết quả ra cho user.
7. User làm việc trực tiếp với từng con, hoặc qua Mike.

**Ràng buộc kỹ thuật bắt buộc (prerequisite — kiểm trước khi build):**
- Claude Code trên server ≥ **v2.1.181** (đủ mọi version-floor: Remote Control 2.1.51, push 2.1.110, Monitor 2.1.98, Agent View 2.1.139, mobile `/config` 2.1.181).
- Đăng nhập **claude.ai OAuth** (gói Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise) trên server. ⚠️ Remote Control **từ chối** API key / `setup-token` / `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`. Phải có một phiên đăng nhập tương tác hợp lệ trên server.
- Server có **outbound HTTPS** tới Anthropic API (không cần inbound — kaffa_v2 sau NAT vẫn OK).
- `tmux` (hoặc systemd user units), `git`, `flock`, `bq` (BigQuery CLI). BQ ghi qua `source wc_env.sh` (cred dtienthanh đã transplant).
- BigQuery: project `[REDACTED]`, dataset `tav2_bq`, location `asia-southeast1`.

---

## 2. Sự thật nền tảng — THẬT vs NGỘ NHẬN

### ✅ THẬT (xây trên các thứ này)
- **Remote Control khả dụng MỌI gói** (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise — Team/Enterprise chỉ cần admin bật toggle). Chạy `claude remote-control` **trên server** → app là cửa sổ, server là máy "local". Outbound-HTTPS, không mở cổng vào.
- **`claude -p` / `--print`** là chế độ headless chính thức (dùng cho cron). Đây mới là "headless" thật.
- **`/loop`**: cron trong-phiên (5 trường cron) — tiện nhưng *session-scoped*, tự xóa sau 7 ngày, chỉ chạy khi phiên idle, chết khi đóng terminal.
- **Hooks** (`SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`, `PreToolUse`…) trong `settings.json` — chạy theo *sự kiện vòng đời* (không định kỳ), do harness chạy ⇒ tin cậy để inject context / heartbeat.
- **Monitor tool** (v2.1.98+): stream từng dòng stdout của tiến trình nền vào phiên *theo sự kiện* (rẻ hơn polling) — cần con stream stdout.
- **Agent Teams** (experimental, cờ `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1`): có mailbox lead↔teammate + task-list chung — **thật** nhưng mong manh (xem dưới).
- **`claude agents`** (Agent View, v2.1.139+): dashboard mở/nhắn mọi phiên nền tại chỗ.
- **BigQuery** đã là hạ tầng sẵn của bạn ⇒ index/registry lý tưởng cho KB.

### ❌ NGỘ NHẬN đã bị bác bỏ (ĐỪNG thiết kế dựa vào)
- **`--teleport`**: KHÔNG tồn tại trong doc. Không có lệnh "pause máy A, resume đầy đủ context máy B rồi bàn giao".
- **"Điện thoại không pair được với claude trên server riêng"**: SAI. Chạy `remote-control` *trên server* CHÍNH LÀ cách pair được hỗ trợ.
- **"Cloud Routine / cloud agent với tay vào phiên server của tôi"**: KHÔNG và KHÔNG THỂ. Routine/Managed-Agent chỉ *outbound-pull*; self-hosted worker poll hàng đợi Anthropic rồi chạy trong **sandbox MỚI mỗi phiên** — không bao giờ attach vào tmux/Remote-Control đang chạy.
- **Cloud Routine làm nhịp 30′**: bất khả — *tối thiểu 1 giờ*.
- **`--headless` / `--daemon`**: cờ không có trong doc chính thức. Dùng `claude -p`.
- **"Hoàn toàn không có nhắn-tin giữa phiên"**: nói quá — Agent Teams có mailbox; nhưng *không* có API chung, ổn định, xuyên-phiên-bất-kỳ, và mailbox đứt qua `/resume`.

### ⚠️ Giới hạn của Agent Teams (lý do KHÔNG làm lõi)
Experimental, tắt mặc định, **một team/phiên, KHÔNG team lồng nhau** (teammate không spawn con của nó ⇒ giết cây Mike→con→cháu), **không resume teammate in-process** (sau `/resume` lead nhắn vào "ma"). ⇒ Dùng làm *garnish live* trên nền bus file/BQ, không bao giờ phụ thuộc nó cho độ bền.

---

## 3. Kiến trúc tổng thể

Xem sơ đồ widget trong chat. Tóm tắt thành phần:

```
[Thiết bị: mobile/desktop/web]  ── chỉ là cửa sổ, rớt mạng vẫn OK
            │  (Remote Control, outbound-HTTPS)
            ▼
==================  SERVER kaffa_v2 (tmux/systemd, luôn chạy)  ==================
   ┌─ Mike (orchestrator) ── claude remote-control --name "Mike"
   │      ▲ giao việc / báo cáo (qua bus)
   │      ▼
   ├─ Agent con #1..#N ── mỗi con: tmux window + git worktree + hooks + remote-control
   │      │ ghi (append-only)        ▲ inject (SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit hook)
   │      ▼                          │
   ├─ BỘ NÃO CHUNG (nguồn sự thật duy nhất):
   │      • message bus file  (bus/inbox/<id>.jsonl — append-only mỗi con)
   │      • BigQuery          (mike_kb_events / mike_kb_index / mike_fleet_registry / mike_directives)
   │      • git               (kb/KNOWLEDGE.md + kb/context_pack.md, single-writer)
   │
   └─ VÒNG LẶP 30′: OS cron → consolidate.sh → claude -p (Mike headless)
          → MERGE BQ → rebuild context_pack → git commit → digest → đánh dấu con "chết"\[BINARY_STRIPPED]
```

**Nguyên lý vàng (rút từ reality-check):**
1. **Conversation là vô thường — state phải externalize.** Không có cloud-store cho phiên local: tmux chết ⇒ hội thoại mất. Mọi thứ bền **phải** nằm ở bus/BQ/git, không nằm trong context phiên nào.
2. **Single-writer + append-only-per-agent.** Bash/Read/Write của Claude Code **không** đảm bảo atomic/locking ⇒ hai con ghi chung 1 file SẼ đua & ghi đè. Mỗi con chỉ ghi **file của riêng nó**; **đúng MỘT** consolidator (Mike-cron) ghi state chung.
3. **Hook kéo, không phải Mike đẩy.** Con tự làm tươi kiến thức qua hook; Mike chỉ xuất bản 1 `context_pack` + bump version.
4. **Supervisor bắt buộc.** Mọi đường device-independent phụ thuộc outbound-HTTPS; mất mạng >~10′ ⇒ Remote Control **timeout & thoát**. Cần systemd `Restart=` / watchdog tự bật lại, nếu không Mike "tắt đèn" âm thầm.

---

## 4. Hạ tầng phiên remote trên server

### 4.1 Mỗi agent = 1 tiến trình bền
Hai lựa chọn (khuyến nghị systemd cho độ bền, tmux cho thao tác tay nhanh):

**A. tmux (đơn giản, thao tác tay):**
```bash
# Trên kaffa_v2
tmux new-session -d -s mike      'cd ~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike && claude remote-control --name "Mike"'
tmux new-session -d -s child-research 'cd ~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/worktrees/research && claude remote-control --name "Con-Research"'
# Ctrl+B D để detach; tmux attach -t mike để vào lại; tiến trình KHÔNG chết khi SSH rớt.
```

**B. systemd user unit (khuyến nghị — tự hồi phục):** `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service`
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Claude agent %i (Mike fleet)
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple\[BINARY_STRIPPED]%i
ExecStart=/usr/bin/claude remote-control --name "%i"
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=20
# Nạp cred BQ cho mọi tiến trình con cần ghi KB:
EnvironmentFile=/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/wc_env.sh

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```
```bash
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable --now mike@Mike
systemctl --user enable --now mike@Con-Research
loginctl enable-linger trido    # giữ user-services chạy cả khi không đăng nhập
```

> ⚠️ **Remote Control cần OAuth tương tác.** systemd chạy `claude remote-control` chỉ hoạt động nếu trên server đã có phiên đăng nhập claude.ai hợp lệ (token còn hạn). Token hết hạn ⇒ cầu nối mobile rớt. → Đưa "kiểm tra & làm tươi đăng nhập" vào checklist vận hành (mục 12).

### 4.2 Truy cập từ mobile/desktop
- Mỗi `remote-control --name "X"` hiện trong **claude.ai/code** và **app Claude** (chấm xanh = online). Mở đúng tên để nói chuyện trực tiếp với con đó.
- Hoặc server-mode 1 tiến trình đỡ nhiều phiên: `claude remote-control --spawn=worktree --capacity 16` (mỗi client 1 git worktree).
- Đóng app/điện thoại bất kỳ lúc nào — server vẫn chạy; mở lại là đồng bộ.

---

## 5. Knowledge Database + Message Bus

### 5.1 Layout thư mục (trên server)
```
~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/
├── MIKE.md                     # "sổ tay vận hành" của Mike (import vào CLAUDE.md của Mike)
├── CHILD_TEMPLATE.md           # sổ tay chuẩn cho agent con
├── kb/
│   ├── KNOWLEDGE.md            # KB hợp nhất, chuẩn tắc (git, CHỈ consolidator ghi)
│   ├── context_pack.md         # snapshot gọn (<10k ký tự) inject vào con
│   └── version.txt             # số version KB đơn điệu tăng
├── bus/
│   ├── inbox/<agent_id>.jsonl       # con ghi event ra (APPEND-ONLY, file riêng mỗi con)
│   ├── directives/<agent_id>.jsonl  # Mike ghi lệnh xuống cho con
│   └── registry/<agent_id>.json     # heartbeat/status (con tự ghi atomic file của mình)
├── locks/consolidator.lock
├── logs/{consolidator.log,heartbeat.log}
├── bin/{append_event.sh, consolidate.sh, spawn_child.sh, publish_context.sh}
└── hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}
```

### 5.2 Vì sao lai file + BigQuery (không SQLite)
- **File JSONL append-only** = đường ghi *nóng*, an toàn (ghi nhỏ atomic), không cần con chạm BQ trực tiếp.
- **BigQuery** = index/registry/lịch sử *nguội*, query mạnh, đã là hạ tầng của bạn. Consolidator `bq load` + `MERGE`.
- **git** = bản chuẩn tắc + audit (diff thấy "ai đổi gì").
- **Bỏ SQLite** (thừa, thêm điểm hỏng).

### 5.3 Schema BigQuery (DDL — chạy 1 lần)
```sql
-- mọi bảng ở tav2_bq (asia-southeast1)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tav2_bq.mike_kb_events (
  event_id   STRING,            -- uuid
  ts         TIMESTAMP,
  agent_id   STRING,
  event_type STRING,            -- finding | status | question | answer | decision | error
  topic      STRING,
  payload    JSON,
  kb_version INT64,
  supersedes STRING             -- event_id bị thay thế (nếu có)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tav2_bq.mike_kb_index (   -- tri thức "hiện hành" đã hợp nhất
  topic       STRING,           -- khóa
  fact        STRING,
  source_agent STRING,
  kb_version  INT64,
  updated_at  TIMESTAMP,
  status      STRING,           -- active | superseded | stale
  ttl_days    INT64
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tav2_bq.mike_fleet_registry (
  agent_id      STRING,         -- cố định, vd "con-research"
  role          STRING,
  session_name  STRING,         -- tên Remote Control
  status        STRING,         -- working | idle | needs_input | dead
  current_task  STRING,
  last_heartbeat TIMESTAMP,
  kb_version    INT64
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tav2_bq.mike_directives (
  directive_id STRING,
  ts          TIMESTAMP,
  target_agent STRING,
  body        STRING,
  status      STRING,           -- open | ack | done
  ack_ts      TIMESTAMP
);
```

### 5.4 Ghi an toàn — helper atomic (`bin/append_event.sh`)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Dùng: append_event.sh <agent_id> <event_type> <topic> <payload_json>
set -euo pipefail
BUS=~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/bus
id="$1"; etype="$2"; topic="$3"; payload="$4"
ts=$(date -u +%FT%TZ)
line=$(jq -cn --arg ts "$ts" --arg a "$id" --arg t "$etype" --arg top "$topic" --argjson p "$payload" \
  '{event_id: (now|tostring), ts:$ts, agent_id:$a, event_type:$t, topic:$top, payload:$p}')
# flock theo TỪNG file con (không tranh chấp giữa các con); append là thao tác nhỏ → atomic trên ext4
exec 9>>"$BUS/inbox/$id.jsonl"; flock 9; printf '%s\n' "$line" >&9
```
Heartbeat (con tự ghi file của mình, atomic bằng temp+rename):
```bash
tmp=$(mktemp); jq -cn --arg s working --arg task "$1" '{status:$s,current_task:$task,ts:(now|tostring)}' >"$tmp"
mv -f "$tmp" "$BUS/registry/$AGENT_ID.json"   # rename = atomic
```

---

## 6. Cập nhật kiến thức cho agent con (không nhắc lại)

Ba lớp, đều **kéo** từ `context_pack.md`:

**(a) CLAUDE.md của con import context-pack** (mỗi worktree con có `CLAUDE.md`):
```markdown
# Con-Research — bối cảnh fleet
@../../kb/context_pack.md
<chỉ dẫn riêng của con này…>
```

**(b) Hook `SessionStart`** (`hooks/session_start.sh`) — kéo bản mới nhất khi mở/resume phiên:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
cd ~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike && git pull --quiet || true
echo '{"continue":true}'
```

**(c) Hook `UserPromptSubmit`** — chèn "có gì mới" nếu version đổi (đây là cốt lõi "con hiểu kết quả con khác"):
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
KB=~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/kb
cur=$(cat "$KB/version.txt"); cache=~/.cache/mike_kbver_$AGENT_ID
seen=$(cat "$cache" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
if [ "$cur" != "$seen" ]; then
  echo "$cur" >"$cache"
  delta=$(sed -n '1,40p' "$KB/context_pack.md")   # phần "ĐÃ ĐỔI" để đầu pack
  printf '{"continue":true,"additionalContext":"KB cập nhật → v%s. Thay đổi gần đây:\\n%s"}' "$cur" "$(echo "$delta" | jq -Rsa . | sed 's/^"//;s/"$//')"
else
  echo '{"continue":true}'
fi
```
Wiring trong `~/.claude/settings.json` (hoặc `.claude/settings.json` trong worktree con):
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart":      [{"matcher":".*","hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/hooks/session_start.sh"}]}],
    "UserPromptSubmit":  [{"matcher":".*","hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/hooks/user_prompt_submit.sh"}]}],
    "Stop":              [{"matcher":".*","hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/hooks/stop.sh"}]}]
  }
}
```

**(d) Hook `Stop`** ở con = vừa heartbeat tự động vừa nhắc ghi KB:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/bin/heartbeat.sh "$AGENT_ID"
echo '{"continue":true,"additionalContext":"Nếu lượt vừa rồi tạo ra tri thức bền (kết luận/quyết định/số liệu), hãy append_event.sh để ghi lên bus trước khi nghỉ."}'
```

> ⚠️ **Rủi ro phải kiểm trên đúng version CC của bạn:** context inject bị **giới hạn ~10k ký tự**, và `SessionStart`/`UserPromptSubmit` **có thể KHÔNG fire cho subagent/teammate** như phiên top-level. ⇒ `context_pack.md` phải là **delta-pointer gọn**, không phải dump cả KB. Test hook có fire không trước khi phụ thuộc.

---

## 7. Nhịp 30 phút (heartbeat + hợp nhất)

**Đồng hồ chính = OS cron** (không phải `/loop`). `crontab -e` trên kaffa_v2:
```cron
# Mike consolidator — mỗi 30 phút (lệch phút :07/:37 để tránh trùng tải)
7,37 * * * * /home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/bin/consolidate.sh >> /home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/logs/consolidator.log 2>&1
```

`bin/consolidate.sh` — 2 tầng (cơ khí xác định + LLM thông minh):
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT=~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike
exec 8>"$ROOT/locks/consolidator.lock"; flock -n 8 || { echo "đang chạy, bỏ qua"; exit 0; }
source ~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/wc_env.sh        # cred BQ ghi

# --- Tầng 1: CƠ KHÍ (deterministic) ---
# 1) nạp event mới từ bus → BigQuery
bq load --source_format=NEWLINE_DELIMITED_JSON tav2_bq.mike_kb_events "$ROOT/bus/inbox/"*.jsonl 2>/dev/null || true
# 2) MERGE vào index; đánh dấu con "chết" nếu heartbeat > 30'
bq query --use_legacy_sql=false < "$ROOT/sql/merge_index.sql"
bq query --use_legacy_sql=false \
 'UPDATE tav2_bq.mike_fleet_registry SET status="dead"
  WHERE TIMESTAMP_DIFF(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), last_heartbeat, MINUTE) > 30 AND status!="dead"'
# 3) cập nhật registry từ bus/registry/*.json (bq load), rebuild context_pack, bump version
"$ROOT/bin/publish_context.sh"
echo $(( $(cat "$ROOT/kb/version.txt") + 1 )) > "$ROOT/kb/version.txt"

# --- Tầng 2: THÔNG MINH (Mike headless) ---
claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions \
 "Đọc tav2_bq.mike_kb_events (30' qua) + mike_fleet_registry. (1) Viết digest fleet 5 dòng vào kb/digest_latest.md;
  (2) Phát hiện tri thức chéo cần phổ biến → thêm vào đầu kb/context_pack.md mục 'ĐÃ ĐỔI';
  (3) Liệt kê con im lặng/chết hoặc mâu thuẫn dữ liệu → ghi directive vào bus/directives/<id>.jsonl." \
 || true

cd "$ROOT" && git add -A && git commit -q -m "consolidate $(date -u +%FT%TZ)" || true
```

**Bổ trợ giữa hai nhịp (tùy chọn):** trong phiên Mike tương tác, dùng **Monitor** stream `tail -f bus/inbox/*.jsonl` để phản ứng *theo sự kiện* (con báo xong/báo lỗi) thay vì chờ tới mốc 30′. Cron 30′ vẫn là đồng hồ bền; Monitor là độ tươi tức thời.

---

## 8. Điều phối & routing (Mike tự trả lời vs giao con)

Cây quyết định khi **user hỏi Mike** (đưa vào `MIKE.md`):
```
1. Tra kb/KNOWLEDGE.md + mike_kb_index + mike_fleet_registry trước.
2. Nếu KB đủ trả lời  → Mike trả lời thẳng, kèm "nguồn: <agent_id>@v<version>".
3. Nếu cần chuyên môn của con X:
   a. Ghi directive: append vào bus/directives/<X>.jsonl (body = câu hỏi, directive_id).
   b. (tùy chọn) Monitor bus/inbox/<X>.jsonl chờ event answer khớp directive_id.
   c. Khi con X trả lời (event 'answer') → Mike tổng hợp, đối chiếu KB, trả ra user.
4. Nếu câu hỏi mơ hồ/chồng nhiều con → Mike phân rã, giao song song, gộp.
5. Việc cần làm ngay nhiều bước → tạo task trong mike_directives + theo dõi status.
```
- `send_message` (giữa-phiên) **chỉ** dùng cho bàn-giao có-người-xác-nhận (vì luôn hỏi xác nhận), KHÔNG dùng cho vòng tự động.
- User cũng có thể **bỏ qua Mike**, mở con X trực tiếp; con vẫn ghi event lên bus ⇒ Mike vẫn thấy ở nhịp 30′.

---

## 9. Vòng đời agent con

```
TẠO       → bin/spawn_child.sh <agent_id> <role>:
              • tạo git worktree mike/agents/<id>
              • copy CHILD_TEMPLATE.md → CLAUDE.md (điền role) + .claude/settings.json (hooks)
              • INSERT mike_fleet_registry (status=idle)
              • systemctl --user enable --now mike@<id>   (mở remote-control có tên)
BRIEF     → Mike ghi directive đầu tiên (mục tiêu, ranh giới file, định nghĩa "xong")
CHẠY      → con làm việc; mỗi lượt: hook inject KB mới; Stop hook heartbeat + nhắc ghi
BÁO CÁO   → con append event finding/answer/status lên bus/inbox/<id>.jsonl
HỢP NHẤT  → cron 30′ kéo event → KB chung → các con khác thấy ở lượt sau
NGHỈ/THU  → Mike đặt status=archived; systemctl --user disable mike@<id>;
            worktree giữ lại để audit; tri thức đã ở KB (không mất khi tắt con)
```
**ID cố định** (vd `con-research`, `con-backtest`) để registry/bus ổn định qua restart.

---

## 10. Template cấu hình

**`MIKE.md`** (sổ tay Mike — import vào CLAUDE.md của Mike):
```markdown
# MIKE — Agent tổng điều phối fleet
Vai trò: đầu mối thông tin; tạo/giám sát/điều phối agent con; giữ KB chung tươi.
Nguồn sự thật: kb/KNOWLEDGE.md, BigQuery mike_*. KHÔNG nhớ trong đầu — luôn tra KB.
Quy tắc:
- Mỗi 30' (cron) hợp nhất bus→KB, sinh digest, phát hiện con chết/mâu thuẫn.
- Routing theo cây ở §8. Tự trả lời nếu KB đủ; nếu không, giao con qua bus/directives.
- Định kỳ rebuild context_pack (mục 'ĐÃ ĐỔI' lên đầu) + bump version để con tự kéo.
- Khi con im lặng > 30' hoặc thiếu ghi KB → ghi directive nhắc.
- Ghi mọi quyết định điều phối thành event 'decision' để audit.
Công cụ: list_sessions/search_session_transcripts (đọc trạng thái con);
         append_event/consolidate/publish_context; bq; Monitor (live).
```

**`CHILD_TEMPLATE.md`** (sổ tay con):
```markdown
# {{ROLE}} — agent con của fleet Mike (id={{AGENT_ID}})
@../../kb/context_pack.md
Quy tắc:
- Trước khi làm: đọc context_pack (đã auto-inject). Không hỏi lại điều KB đã ghi.
- Khi có kết luận/số liệu/quyết định bền → append_event.sh "{{AGENT_ID}}" finding "<topic>" '<json>'.
- Đọc bus/directives/{{AGENT_ID}}.jsonl để nhận việc từ Mike; trả lời = event 'answer' kèm directive_id.
- Mỗi phiên: Stop hook tự heartbeat; nếu tạo tri thức mà quên ghi, hook sẽ nhắc.
- Phạm vi file: chỉ trong worktree của mình (tránh đụng con khác).
```

---

## 11. Lộ trình triển khai (3 phase)

**Phase 1 — Xương sống device-independent + bus (1 buổi):**
1. Nâng CC ≥2.1.181 trên kaffa_v2; đăng nhập claude.ai OAuth; kiểm `claude remote-control --name Test` → mở được trên mobile.
2. Dựng cây thư mục `mike/`, `bin/append_event.sh`, `heartbeat.sh`, hooks; git init.
3. Mike + 1 con qua systemd user-unit; nói chuyện được từ điện thoại với cả hai.
4. Cron 30′ phiên bản *cơ khí* (chưa LLM): load bus→file, rebuild context_pack, bump version, git commit.
→ *Nghiệm thu:* con ghi event, 30′ sau con khác thấy delta ở lượt mới.

**Phase 2 — BigQuery + Mike thông minh:**
5. Tạo 4 bảng `mike_*`; `merge_index.sql`; tầng 2 `claude -p` sinh digest + directive.
6. Routing §8 trong `MIKE.md`; test Mike-tự-trả-lời vs Mike-giao-con.
7. Watchdog systemd `Restart=` + alert khi `workers/agent dead`.
→ *Nghiệm thu:* hỏi Mike từ mobile → Mike định tuyến xuống con → trả kết quả ra.

**Phase 3 — Tiện ích & độ bền:**
8. Monitor live giữa hai nhịp; PushNotification khi có việc cần quyết.
9. (Tùy chọn) bật Agent Teams thử nghiệm cho công việc cần nhắn-tin live, KHÔNG bỏ bus.
10. Load-test số con đồng thời; chốt `--capacity`; pin version; tài liệu vận hành.

---

## 12. Vận hành, giám sát, phục hồi

- **Watchdog:** systemd `Restart=on-failure` + 1 cron phụ kiểm `systemctl --user is-active mike@*`; nếu chết → bật lại + PushNotification.
- **Mất mạng >10′:** Remote Control timeout & thoát → `Restart=` bật lại; báo cho user "cầu nối vừa phục hồi".
- **OAuth hết hạn:** lịch nhắc kiểm đăng nhập claude.ai trên server (token là điểm yếu cố hữu của Remote Control).
- **KB đua ghi:** chỉ 1 consolidator (flock `-n`), con chỉ ghi file riêng. Không bao giờ 2 writer 1 file.
- **Con chạy KB cũ:** kiểm hook fire trên đúng version; nếu subagent không nhận inject → ép đọc context_pack ở dòng đầu directive.
- **Chi phí/tải:** mỗi con giữ 1 remote-control + context; nhịp 30′ đọc lại nhiều ⇒ theo dõi rate-limit & RAM/CPU. Chưa có benchmark số phiên remote-control đồng thời/host → load-test trước khi mở rộng.

---

## 13. Rủi ro & giới hạn (thẳng thắn)

| Rủi ro | Mức | Giảm thiểu |
|---|---|---|
| Mọi đường phụ thuộc outbound-HTTPS; mất mạng >10′ giết Remote Control | Cao | systemd Restart + watchdog + alert |
| Phiên local KHÔNG có cloud-store; tmux/server chết = hội thoại mất | Cao | State bền 100% ở bus/BQ/git; coi conversation là vô thường |
| Agent Teams experimental, đứt qua /resume, không lồng nhau | TB | Chỉ dùng làm lớp live; lõi là bus file/BQ |
| Đua ghi KB (CC không atomic/lock) | TB | append-only mỗi con + 1 consolidator + flock + temp/rename |
| Inject context cap ~10k & có thể không fire cho subagent | TB | context_pack = delta gọn; test hook trên đúng version |
| Remote Control bắt buộc OAuth tương tác (không API key) | TB | quy trình làm tươi token; coi là liability vận hành |
| Version fragility (mọi thứ research-preview) | TB | pin version CC; re-validate sau mỗi nâng cấp |
| `send_message` luôn hỏi xác nhận → không tự động hóa được | Thấp | điều phối qua bus, không qua send_message |
| Cloud Routine ≥1 giờ, không với tay vào phiên server | Thấp | dùng OS cron, không dùng Cloud Routine cho lõi |

---

## 14. Phụ lục — lệnh & version-floor

- `claude remote-control --name "X" [--spawn=worktree] [--capacity 16]` — phiên server có tên, hiện trên mobile/web.
- `claude -p "<prompt>"` — headless (dùng cho cron). `--dangerously-skip-permissions` cho chạy không-người.
- `claude agents` — dashboard mọi phiên nền (v2.1.139+).
- `/loop 30m "<prompt>"` — cron trong-phiên (tiện, KHÔNG bền — chỉ dùng phụ).
- Version-floor: Remote Control 2.1.51 · Monitor 2.1.98 · push 2.1.110 · Agent View 2.1.139 · mobile `/config` 2.1.181 · Agent Teams = cờ `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1`.
- BQ: `source ~/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/wc_env.sh` rồi `bq query --use_legacy_sql=false ...` (asia-southeast1).

---
*Nguồn: workflow xác minh năng lực Claude Code + reality-check, [REDACTED]21. Mọi cơ chế "THẬT" đã đối chiếu doc; mọi "NGỘ NHẬN" đã bị bác bỏ có chủ đích.*

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- kronos: Forecast financial candlesticks (K-lines) with Kronos — the open-source foundation model pre-trained on 12B+ K-line records from 45 exchanges (arXiv 2508.02739, AAAI 2026). Use when the user wants a model-based price/volatility forecast or "market language" read on a ticker, an index, or a CSV of OHLCV data. Pulls data from BigQuery (ticker / ticker_1m) or a local CSV, runs Kronos zero-shot, and outputs a forecast table + chart.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review this plan carefully. It's a sophisticated design and most of it hinges on specific Claude Code capabilities, so let me verify the load-bearing technical claims and check the actual state of your server in parallel before giving feedback.

input.description: Verify Claude Code capabilities
input.prompt:
I'm reviewing an architecture plan that depends on specific Claude Code (CLI) capabilities. Please verify each claim below against current Claude Code documentation/behavior and tell me which are TRUE, FALSE, or UNCERTAIN, with brief reasoning. Be precise about version floors where you can.

1. `claude remote-control` exists as a command. Running it ON a server puts that server's Claude session into a "server mode" that becomes reachable from the Claude mobile app and claude.ai/code, using only OUTBOUND HTTPS (no inbound port needed). It supports flags `--name "X"`, `--spawn=worktree`, and `--capacity N`.

2. Remote Control REQUIRES interactive claude.ai OAuth login (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise) and REJECTS API key / setup-token / CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN auth.

3. If the network drops for ~10 minutes, a Remote Control session times out and exits.

4. `claude -p "<prompt>"` (a.k.a. `--print`) is the official headless/non-interactive mode, suitable for running from OS cron. `--dangerously-skip-permissions` lets it run unattended.

5. Hooks `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`, `PreToolUse` exist and are configured in settings.json. They are executed by the harness (not the model). A `UserPromptSubmit` hook can inject additional context into the conversation each turn via JSON output like `{"continue":true,"additionalContext":"..."}`.

6. Whether SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit hooks fire for SUBAGENTS / Agent-Teams teammates the same way they fire for a top-level session. (This is flagged as uncertain in the plan — please confirm.)

7. There is a context-injection size cap around ~10k characters for hook-injected additionalContext.

8. `send_message` (between-session messaging) [REDACTED] prompts for user confirmation and cannot be used in fully-automated/headless flows.

9. `/loop` is an in-session recurring command (cron-like), is session-scoped, auto-deletes after ~7 days, and does not survive closing the session/terminal.

10. There is NO `--teleport`, `--headless`, or `--daemon` flag in Claude Code.

11. Cloud Routines / scheduled cloud agents have a MINIMUM interval of 1 hour (cannot do every 30 minutes), and cannot attach into a running local tmux/Remote-Control session.

12. A `Monitor` capability exists (v2.1.98+) that streams a background process's stdout into a session event-by-event.

13. `claude agents` is a dashboard (Agent View, v2.1.139+) for managing background sessions.

14. The version floors claimed: Remote Control 2.1.51, Monitor 2.1.98, push 2.1.110, Agent View 2.1.139, mobile /config 2.1.181, Agent Teams behind env flag CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1.

toolUseResult.prompt:
I'm reviewing an architecture plan that depends on specific Claude Code (CLI) capabilities. Please verify each claim below against current Claude Code documentation/behavior and tell me which are TRUE, FALSE, or UNCERTAIN, with brief reasoning. Be precise about version floors where you can.

1. `claude remote-control` exists as a command. Running it ON a server puts that server's Claude session into a "server mode" that becomes reachable from the Claude mobile app and claude.ai/code, using only OUTBOUND HTTPS (no inbound port needed). It supports flags `--name "X"`, `--spawn=worktree`, and `--capacity N`.

2. Remote Control REQUIRES interactive claude.ai OAuth login (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise) and REJECTS API key / setup-token / CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN auth.

3. If the network drops for ~10 minutes, a Remote Control session times out and exits.

4. `claude -p "<prompt>"` (a.k.a. `--print`) is the official headless/non-interactive mode, suitable for running from OS cron. `--dangerously-skip-permissions` lets it run unattended.

5. Hooks `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`, `PreToolUse` exist and are configured in settings.json. They are executed by the harness (not the model). A `UserPromptSubmit` hook can inject additional context into the conversation each turn via JSON output like `{"continue":true,"additionalContext":"..."}`.

6. Whether SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit hooks fire for SUBAGENTS / Agent-Teams teammates the same way they fire for a top-level session. (This is flagged as uncertain in the plan — please confirm.)

7. There is a context-injection size cap around ~10k characters for hook-injected additionalContext.

8. `send_message` (between-session messaging) [REDACTED] prompts for user confirmation and cannot be used in fully-automated/headless flows.

9. `/loop` is an in-session recurring command (cron-like), is session-scoped, auto-deletes after ~7 days, and does not survive closing the session/terminal.

10. There is NO `--teleport`, `--headless`, or `--daemon` flag in Claude Code.

11. Cloud Routines / scheduled cloud agents have a MINIMUM interval of 1 hour (cannot do every 30 minutes), and cannot attach into a running local tmux/Remote-Control session.

12. A `Monitor` capability exists (v2.1.98+) that streams a background process's stdout into a session event-by-event.

13. `claude agents` is a dashboard (Agent View, v2.1.139+) for managing background sessions.

14. The version floors claimed: Remote Control 2.1.51, Monitor 2.1.98, push 2.1.110, Agent View 2.1.139, mobile /config 2.1.181, Agent Teams behind env flag CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1.

Also: please report what `claude --version` returns on this machine if you can run it, and whether `claude remote-control --help` shows the flags above.

Return a concise table: claim # → TRUE/FALSE/UNCERTAIN → one-line reason.

aiTitle: Xây dựng hệ thống Mike orchestrator quản lý agents

content.text:
I've verified the plan's technical claims against current Claude Code behavior and checked your server. Here's what I found before I give you my review.

**Verification results (claude-code-guide, against current docs):**
- ✅ Almost everything load-bearing checks out: `remote-control` server-mode + flags, OAuth-required (rejects API keys), ~10-min network-drop timeout, `claude -p` headless, the four hooks + `additionalContext` injection, `send_message` [REDACTED]-confirms, `/loop` session-scoped 7-day, Cloud Routines ≥1h and can't attach to local sessions, no `--teleport/--headless/--daemon`.
- ⚠️ **Claim #6 (hooks fire for subagents) — confirmed UNCERTAIN.** This is the one real risk in the plan, and it's load-bearing.
- ❌ **Claim #7 (~10k char inject cap) — NOT documented.** No stated cap. The plan over-worries here (harmless, but the constraint is imaginary).
- Agent Teams env-flag name and Monitor's exact version floor aren't doc-confirmed (minor).

**Server reality (better than the plan assumes):**
- Claude CLI **v2.1.185** — already above the 2.1.181 floor. Cloud-enabled, outbound OK.
- `tmux`, `jq`, `flock`, `bq` (via `wc_env.sh`), `git` all present. Remote: `github.com/minhtrido2023/thanhdt.git`.
- `mike/` does **not** exist yet — greenfield.
- ⚠️ **Linger = OFF** → user services die on logout; needs `loginctl enable-linger trido`.
- ⚠️ **6 production cron jobs already exist** (papertrade, telegram, v34b refresh…). New cron must be *added*, and watch for BQ contention with the 16:15 refresh.

---

## My verdict

The plan is **technically sound and unusually well-researched** — the THẬT/NGỘ-NHẬN section is correct, and the core architecture (remote-control for device-independence · state externalized to bus/BQ/git · OS cron as the durable clock · single-writer/append-per-agent) is the right spine. I'd build on it.

But it has **one conceptual gap and a few concrete bugs** worth resolving before you commit:

**🔴 The conceptual gap — Mike cannot autonomously wake an idle child.** A remote-control child is an *interactive* session. `send_message` [REDACTED] asks for confirmation (verified), so Mike has **no unattended way to push work into an idle child**. A child reads `bus/directives/<id>.jsonl` only when something prompts it. So as written, "Mike giao việc cho con và con tự làm" doesn't actually work unattended — children act only when *you* (a human) interact with them. To get true autonomous dispatch, each child needs its *own* headless `claude -p` loop pulling its queue — which makes children **workers**, a different design from interactive companions. The plan blends the two. This is the key decision.

**🟠 Concrete bugs in `consolidate.sh`:**
1. `bq load ... bus/inbox/*.jsonl` runs over the **full files every 30 min** → re-ingests every event each cycle → `mike_kb_events` fills with duplicates. Fix: load to a staging table + `MERGE` on `event_id`, or rotate/offset processed lines.
2. `event_id = (now|tostring)` is **seconds-resolution → collisions**; two events in one second clash (and then dedup drops real ones). Use a real UUID.

**🟠 Operational liabilities the plan under-mitigates:**
- **OAuth expiry** kills the whole bridge + all children, and the only fix is interactive re-login. "A reminder" is too weak — needs a watchdog → PushNotification. Worth pinning down the token lifetime first.
- **The 30-min autonomous `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions`** is a fully autonomous Claude with write access to the shared context_pack + directives, 48×/day forever. It can hallucinate a "cross-finding" and poison every child's context. Keep Tier-2 strictly additive/bounded/audited, never let it rewrite `KNOWLEDGE.md`, and note the mechanical Tier-1 already gives Mike fleet-status every 30 min — the *expensive* LLM digest doesn't need that cadence.
- **Complexity/ROI:** 4 BQ tables + file bus + git + 4 hooks + 4 scripts + systemd templating + 2 cron tiers + watchdog is a lot of failure points for one user. The phasing is good; I'd push harder on "ship Phase-1 spine, live with it, only add BQ + autonomous-Mike if the file-only version proves insufficient."

Before I write this up as a concrete go-forward plan, two of these are genuinely your call:

questions.question: Khi Mike "giao việc cho agent con", bạn kỳ vọng con hoạt động thế nào? (Đây là quyết định kiến trúc quan trọng nhất — remote-control một mình KHÔNG cho Mike đánh thức con đang idle để tự chạy việc.)

options.label: Companion tương tác
options.description: Con là phiên remote-control bạn tự điều khiển từ mobile. Mike chủ yếu định tuyến câu hỏi + tổng hợp KB mỗi 30'. Directive là 'lời nhắc', con đọc khi bạn tương tác. Khớp remote-control sạch sẽ nhất, đơn giản nhất.

options.label: Worker tự động
options.description: Con chạy việc KHÔNG cần người. Mike giao việc, con tự thực thi. Bắt buộc mỗi con có thêm một vòng lặp headless 'claude -p' riêng kéo hàng đợi directive. Phức tạp hơn nhiều, token cao hơn.

options.label: Hybrid (cả hai)

options.description: Thêm 4 bảng mike_* + MERGE index ngay, nhưng tầng 2 (claude -p tự sinh digest/directive) để sau. Có lịch sử SQL nhưng tránh agent tự trị ghi context.

aiTitle: Xây dựng hệ thống Mike orchestrator quản lý agents

content.text:
Your three choices land on the cleanest, lowest-risk version of this system, and they're mutually consistent:

- **Companion tương tác** → children are interactive remote-control sessions; Mike routes + aggregates; no need for per-child headless workers. This *removes* the conceptual gap entirely (we're not asking Mike to wake idle children).
- **Phase-1 spine first** → file bus + mechanical cron only.
- **Git markdown KB first** → no BigQuery, no `bq-load` duplication bug to fix, no autonomous `claude -p` writing shared context.

Net effect: the build shrinks to a well-understood spine, and the two scariest items (autonomous write-access Mike every 30 min; BigQuery dedup) are simply deferred. Let me write this up.

input.content:
# Mike Fleet — Phase-1 Spine (companion model, git-KB, no BigQuery)

## Context

You want a "Mike" orchestrator session on the **kaffa_v2** server that creates and coordinates child
Claude Code agents, all running as **remote-control** sessions so you can drive them from Claude Code
mobile/desktop without interruption, and have everything survive device disconnects. The full vision is
captured in `MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md` (uploaded). That plan is technically sound — its capability
research was re-verified against current Claude Code behavior ([REDACTED]21) and holds up.

This plan file is the **reviewed, de-risked starting point**. Based on your three decisions:

1. **Companion model** — children are interactive remote-control sessions *you* drive. Mike routes
   questions and aggregates knowledge; it does **not** autonomously dispatch work into idle children.
   (This is the correct call: `send_message` [REDACTED] asks for confirmation, so remote-control alone
   gives Mike no unattended way to wake an idle child — the companion model sidesteps that entirely.)
2. **Phase-1 spine first** — file message-bus + mechanical cron. No BigQuery, no autonomous LLM Mike.
3. **Git-markdown KB first** — `KNOWLEDGE.md` + `context_pack.md` in git, JSONL bus. Add BigQuery later
   only if SQL history/analytics across many agents is actually needed.

Goal of this phase: prove the spine — a child writes a finding, ≥30 min later another child sees it as
injected "what's new" context without anyone repeating it, and you can talk to Mike and any child from
your phone while the server keeps running.

## Verified facts that shape this plan

- Server already has Claude CLI **v2.1.185** (> 2.1.181 floor), `tmux` 3.2a, `jq`, `flock`, `bq`, `git`.
  Outbound HTTPS works. Remote: `github.com/minhtrido2023/thanhdt.git`.
- `mike/` does **not** exist yet — greenfield under `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/`.
- **Linger is OFF** — must run `loginctl enable-linger trido` or systemd user units die on logout.
- **6 production cron jobs already exist** (papertrade 8:30, pt_8l 10:45, telegram 11:00, v34b refresh
  16:15, commodity 1st/10th). The new consolidator must be *added* and scheduled to avoid the 16:15
  BQ-heavy window. The `:07/:37` offset in the original plan is fine.
- Hooks injecting `additionalContext` have **no documented ~10k char cap** (original plan over-worried;
  keep `context_pack.md` lean anyway, for cost/latency, not because of a hard cap).
- Whether hooks fire for *subagents* is **uncertain** — but irrelevant here: every child is a
  **top-level remote-control session**, where hooks fire reliably. Do **not** make children Agent-tool
  subagents.

## Corrections folded in from the original plan

- **No `bq load` in this phase** → the duplicate-ingestion bug (re-loading full JSONL every 30 min) and
  the seconds-resolution `event_id` collision both disappear. When BigQuery is added later, load events
  into a staging table and `MERGE` on `event_id`, and generate `event_id` with a real UUID
  (`cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid`), not `(now|tostring)`.
- **Cron Tier-2 (autonomous `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions`) is deferred.** The 30-min cron in
  this phase is **mechanical only**: collect heartbeats → registry, rebuild `context_pack.md` from recent
  finding events, bump version, git commit. The *smart* digest/synthesis is done by **Mike interactively,
  on demand** when you ask — not by an unattended agent with write access to shared context.

## Directory layout (greenfield, under `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/`)

```
mike/
├── MIKE.md                      # Mike's operating handbook (imported into Mike's CLAUDE.md)
├── CHILD_TEMPLATE.md            # child handbook template
├── kb/
│   ├── KNOWLEDGE.md             # canonical KB (git; consolidator-written only)
│   ├── context_pack.md          # lean snapshot injected into children
│   └── version.txt              # monotonically increasing KB version
├── bus/
│   ├── inbox/<id>.jsonl         # each child appends its own events (append-only, one file per child)
│   ├── directives/<id>.jsonl    # Mike writes advisory directives for a child
│   └── registry/<id>.json       # heartbeat/status (child writes its own file atomically)
├── agents/<id>/                 # per-child git worktree (CLAUDE.md + .claude/settings.json)
├── locks/consolidator.lock
├── logs/{consolidator.log,watchdog.log}
├── bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}
└── hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}
```

## Build steps

### 1. Prereqs (one-time)
- `loginctl enable-linger trido` (so user services survive logout/reboot — currently OFF).
- Confirm an interactive **claude.ai OAuth** login exists on the server (Remote Control rejects API
  keys / tokens). `claude remote-control --name Test` should appear online in the mobile app. Record
  the OAuth token lifetime — it is the system's #1 operational liability (see Risks).
- `mkdir -p mike/{kb,bus/{inbox,directives,registry},agents,locks,logs,bin,hooks}` and `git`-track it
  inside the existing repo.

### 2. Bus helpers (`bin/`)
- `append_event.sh <id> <event_type> <topic> <payload_json>` — per the original plan, but generate
  `event_id` with `cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid`. `flock` on the child's own file; append is atomic.
- `heartbeat.sh <id> <task>` — writes `bus/registry/<id>.json` via temp-file + `mv` (atomic rename).

### 3. Hooks (`hooks/`) — wired in each child's `agents/<id>/.claude/settings.json`
- `session_start.sh` — `git -C mike pull --quiet || true` then `{"continue":true}`.
- `user_prompt_submit.sh` — the core of "children see each other's results": compare
  `kb/version.txt` to a per-agent cache; if changed, inject the top "RECENT" section of
  `context_pack.md` as `additionalContext` and update the cache. (Keep `AGENT_ID` exported per child.)
- `stop.sh` — call `heartbeat.sh`, then inject the reminder to `append_event.sh` any durable knowledge
  produced this turn.

### 4. Mechanical consolidator (`bin/consolidate.sh` + `publish_context.sh`)
- `flock -n` on `locks/consolidator.lock` (single writer — never two).
- Read all `bus/registry/*.json` → mark any child with no heartbeat in >30 min as `dead` in a status
  view; collect recent `finding`/`answer`/`decision` events from `bus/inbox/*.jsonl`.
- `publish_context.sh` rebuilds `kb/context_pack.md` with a **"RECENT (since last version)"** section at
  the very top (this is what the hook injects as the delta), followed by stable pointers into
  `KNOWLEDGE.md`. Bump `kb/version.txt`. `git add -A && git commit`.
- **No `claude -p` in this phase.** Synthesis into `KNOWLEDGE.md` is done by Mike interactively.

### 5. Cron (add to existing crontab, do not replace the 6 jobs)
```
7,37 * * * * /home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/bin/consolidate.sh >> .../logs/consolidator.log 2>&1
```
Verify no overlap/contention with the 16:15 `daily_refresh_v34b_linux.sh`.

### 6. Sessions (systemd user units — durable, auto-restart)
- `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service` with `ExecStart=/usr/bin/claude remote-control --name "%i"`,
  `Restart=on-failure`, `RestartSec=20`, `WorkingDirectory=.../mike/agents/%i`.
- **Pick one model explicitly: one named remote-control session per child** (`mike@Mike`,
  `mike@Con-Research`, …). Drop the `--spawn=worktree --capacity` alternative for now — it's for ad-hoc
  client worktrees, not a persistent named fleet, and mixing the two only confuses operations.
- `spawn_child.sh <id> <role>`: create git worktree `agents/<id>`, render `CHILD_TEMPLATE.md` →
  `agents/<id>/CLAUDE.md` (imports `../../kb/context_pack.md`) + `.claude/settings.json` (the 3 hooks),
  write initial `registry/<id>.json`, then `systemctl --user enable --now mike@<id>`.

### 7. Watchdog (`bin/watchdog.sh`, small cron, e.g. every 10 min)
- Check `systemctl --user is-active mike@*`; restart any dead unit.
- Detect a dead bridge / likely-expired OAuth (e.g. unit flapping, or a probe that the remote-control
  session is offline) → fire a **PushNotification** so you know to re-auth. (Manual "reminder" from the
  original plan is too weak; make the failure *tell you*.)

## Critical files to create
- `mike/bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}`
- `mike/hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}`
- `mike/{MIKE.md, CHILD_TEMPLATE.md}`, `mike/kb/{KNOWLEDGE.md, context_pack.md, version.txt}`
- `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service`
- crontab entries (consolidator + watchdog)

Script *bodies* are in `MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md` §5–§7 and §10 — reuse them with the two corrections
above (UUID `event_id`; consolidator is mechanical-only this phase).

## Verification (acceptance for Phase-1)

1. `claude remote-control --name Mike` and one child both show online in the **mobile app**; you can chat
   with each directly. Kill your phone's network, reconnect → sessions still there (server kept running).
2. From the child session, produce a finding → `append_event.sh con-research finding "<topic>" '<json>'`
   writes to `bus/inbox/con-research.jsonl`.
3. Wait for the `:07/:37` cron (or run `consolidate.sh` by hand): `context_pack.md` gains a RECENT entry,
   `version.txt` increments, a git commit appears.
4. In a **second** child, submit any prompt → the `UserPromptSubmit` hook injects "KB updated → vN,
   recent changes: …" **without anyone repeating it**. This is the headline proof.
5. `reboot` the server (or `systemctl --user restart`) → units come back via linger + `Restart=`;
   registry shows heartbeats resume.
6. Stop a child for >30 min → consolidator marks it `dead` in the status view; watchdog/PushNotification
   behaves as configured.

## Explicitly deferred (Phase 2+, only if Phase-1 proves insufficient)
- BigQuery KB (4 `mike_*` tables, MERGE index) — add **with** staging-table + `MERGE`-on-UUID dedup.
- Autonomous Tier-2 `claude -p` digest/directive generation — keep strictly additive, bounded, audited;
  never let it rewrite `KNOWLEDGE.md`; consider hourly (not 30-min) cadence.
- Agent Teams live mailbox (experimental) — garnish only, never load-bearing.
- Monitor-based live reactions between 30-min ticks.

## Risks carried into this phase
- **OAuth expiry** kills the bridge + all children; fix is interactive re-login. Mitigation: watchdog →
  PushNotification, and know the token lifetime up front.
- **Network drop >~10 min** times out remote-control; `Restart=` brings it back, but the in-flight
  conversation is lost — all durable state must already be on the bus/git (it is, by design).
- **Companion-model limit (accepted):** Mike cannot make an idle child do background work unattended.
  Children act when you interact with them; directives are advisory, consumed on next interaction. If you
  later want true autonomous dispatch, that's the Hybrid model (per-child headless `claude -p` worker) —
  a Phase-3 decision, not this phase.

```
├── bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}
└── hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}
```

## Build steps

### 1. Prereqs (one-time)
- `loginctl enable-linger trido` (so user services survive logout/reboot — currently OFF).
- Confirm an interactive **claude.ai OAuth** login exists on the server (Remote Control rejects API
  keys / tokens). `claude remote-control --name Test` should appear online in the mobile app. Record
  the OAuth token lifetime — it is the system's #1 operational liability (see Risks).
- `mkdir -p mike/{kb,bus/{inbox,directives,registry},agents,locks,logs,bin,hooks}` and `git`-track it
  inside the existing repo.

### 2. Bus helpers (`bin/`)
- `append_event.sh <id> <event_type> <topic> <payload_json>` — per the original plan, but generate
  `event_id` with `cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid`. `flock` on the child's own file; append is atomic.
- `heartbeat.sh <id> <task>` — writes `bus/registry/<id>.json` via temp-file + `mv` (atomic rename).

### 3. Hooks (`hooks/`) — wired in each child's `agents/<id>/.claude/settings.json`
- `session_start.sh` — `git -C mike pull --quiet || true` then `{"continue":true}`.
- `user_prompt_submit.sh` — the core of "children see each other's results": compare
  `kb/version.txt` to a per-agent cache; if changed, inject the top "RECENT" section of
  `context_pack.md` as `additionalContext` and update the cache. (Keep `AGENT_ID` exported per child.)
- `stop.sh` — call `heartbeat.sh`, then inject the reminder to `append_event.sh` any durable knowledge
  produced this turn.

### 4. Mechanical consolidator (`bin/consolidate.sh` + `publish_context.sh`)
- `flock -n` on `locks/consolidator.lock` (single writer — never two).
- Read all `bus/registry/*.json` → mark any child with no heartbeat in >30 min as `dead` in a status
  view; collect recent `finding`/`answer`/`decision` events from `bus/inbox/*.jsonl`.
- `publish_context.sh` rebuilds `kb/context_pack.md` with a **"RECENT (since last version)"** section at
  the very top (this is what the hook injects as the delta), followed by stable pointers into
  `KNOWLEDGE.md`. Bump `kb/version.txt`. `git add -A && git commit`.
- **No `claude -p` in this phase.** Synthesis into `KNOWLEDGE.md` is done by Mike interactively.

### 5. Cron (add to existing crontab, do not replace the 6 jobs)
```
7,37 * * * * /home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/bin/consolidate.sh >> .../logs/consolidator.log 2>&1
```
Verify no overlap/contention with the 16:15 `daily_refresh_v34b_linux.sh`.

### 6. Sessions (systemd user units — durable, auto-restart)
- `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service` with `ExecStart=/usr/bin/claude remote-control --name "%i"`,
  `Restart=on-failure`, `RestartSec=20`, `WorkingDirectory=.../mike/agents/%i`.
- **Pick one model explicitly: one named remote-control session per child** (`mike@Mike`,
  `mike@Con-Research`, …). Drop the `--spawn=worktree --capacity` alternative for now — it's for ad-hoc
  client worktrees, not a persistent named fleet, and mixing the two only confuses operations.
- `spawn_child.sh <id> <role>`: create git worktree `agents/<id>`, render `CHILD_TEMPLATE.md` →
  `agents/<id>/CLAUDE.md` (imports `../../kb/context_pack.md`) + `.claude/settings.json` (the 3 hooks),
  write initial `registry/<id>.json`, then `systemctl --user enable --now mike@<id>`.

### 7. Watchdog (`bin/watchdog.sh`, small cron, e.g. every 10 min)
- Check `systemctl --user is-active mike@*`; restart any dead unit.
- Detect a dead bridge / likely-expired OAuth (e.g. unit flapping, or a probe that the remote-control
  session is offline) → fire a **PushNotification** so you know to re-auth. (Manual "reminder" from the
  original plan is too weak; make the failure *tell you*.)

## Critical files to create
- `mike/bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}`
- `mike/hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}`
- `mike/{MIKE.md, CHILD_TEMPLATE.md}`, `mike/kb/{KNOWLEDGE.md, context_pack.md, version.txt}`
- `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service`
- crontab entries (consolidator + watchdog)

Script *bodies* are in `MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md` §5–§7 and §10 — reuse them with the two corrections
above (UUID `event_id`; consolidator is mechanical-only this phase).

## Verification (acceptance for Phase-1)

1. `claude remote-control --name Mike` and one child both show online in the **mobile app**; you can chat
   with each directly. Kill your phone's network, reconnect → sessions still there (server kept running).
2. From the child session, produce a finding → `append_event.sh con-research finding "<topic>" '<json>'`
   writes to `bus/inbox/con-research.jsonl`.
3. Wait for the `:07/:37` cron (or run `consolidate.sh` by hand): `context_pack.md` gains a RECENT entry,
   `version.txt` increments, a git commit appears.
4. In a **second** child, submit any prompt → the `UserPromptSubmit` hook injects "KB updated → vN,
   recent changes: …" **without anyone repeating it**. This is the headline proof.
5. `reboot` the server (or `systemctl --user restart`) → units come back via linger + `Restart=`;
   registry shows heartbeats resume.
6. Stop a child for >30 min → consolidator marks it `dead` in the status view; watchdog/PushNotification
   behaves as configured.

## Explicitly deferred (Phase 2+, only if Phase-1 proves insufficient)
- BigQuery KB (4 `mike_*` tables, MERGE index) — add **with** staging-table + `MERGE`-on-UUID dedup.
- Autonomous Tier-2 `claude -p` digest/directive generation — keep strictly additive, bounded, audited;
  never let it rewrite `KNOWLEDGE.md`; consider hourly (not 30-min) cadence.
- Agent Teams live mailbox (experimental) — garnish only, never load-bearing.
- Monitor-based live reactions between 30-min ticks.

## Risks carried into this phase
- **OAuth expiry** kills the bridge + all children; fix is interactive re-login. Mitigation: watchdog →
  PushNotification, and know the token lifetime up front.
- **Network drop >~10 min** times out remote-control; `Restart=` brings it back, but the in-flight
  conversation is lost — all durable state must already be on the bus/git (it is, by design).
- **Companion-model limit (accepted):** Mike cannot make an idle child do background work unattended.
  Children act when you interact with them; directives are advisory, consumed on next interaction. If you
  later want true autonomous dispatch, that's the Hybrid model (per-child headless `claude -p` worker) —
  a Phase-3 decision, not this phase.

content.text:
I've written the reviewed plan. Summary of where this landed:

**The original plan is good** — its capability research is accurate (I re-verified it). I'm not rewriting it; I'm de-risking the *starting point* based on your three choices, which together remove the two scariest items (autonomous write-access Mike every 30 min, and the BigQuery `bq-load` duplication bug) by simply deferring them.

**What changed from the upload:**
- **Companion model** eliminates the one real conceptual gap (Mike can't wake idle children unattended — so we don't ask it to).
- **Cron is mechanical-only** this phase: collect heartbeats → rebuild `context_pack` → bump version → git commit. The smart synthesis is Mike-interactive-on-demand, not an unattended autonomous agent.
- **Git-markdown KB**, no BigQuery → the duplicate-event and `event_id` collision bugs never arise (and I noted the fixes for when BQ is added later).
- Folded in the server reality: linger is OFF (must enable), 6 prod crons already exist (add, don't replace), CLI v2.1.185 already meets the floor, and the imaginary ~10k inject cap is dropped.
- Made the OAuth-expiry liability *tell you* via watchdog → PushNotification instead of a manual reminder, and pinned children as **top-level sessions** so the uncertain "hooks-fire-for-subagents" question never bites.

The headline acceptance test is step 4: a second child sees the first child's finding as injected "what's new" context with nobody repeating it.

# Mike Fleet — Phase-1 Spine (companion model, git-KB, no BigQuery)

## Context

You want a "Mike" orchestrator session on the **kaffa_v2** server that creates and coordinates child
Claude Code agents, all running as **remote-control** sessions so you can drive them from Claude Code
mobile/desktop without interruption, and have everything survive device disconnects. The full vision is
captured in `MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md` (uploaded). That plan is technically sound — its capability
research was re-verified against current Claude Code behavior ([REDACTED]21) and holds up.

This plan file is the **reviewed, de-risked starting point**. Based on your three decisions:

1. **Companion model** — children are interactive remote-control sessions *you* drive. Mike routes
   questions and aggregates knowledge; it does **not** autonomously dispatch work into idle children.
   (This is the correct call: `send_message` [REDACTED] asks for confirmation, so remote-control alone
   gives Mike no unattended way to wake an idle child — the companion model sidesteps that entirely.)
2. **Phase-1 spine first** — file message-bus + mechanical cron. No BigQuery, no autonomous LLM Mike.
3. **Git-markdown KB first** — `KNOWLEDGE.md` + `context_pack.md` in git, JSONL bus. Add BigQuery later
   only if SQL history/analytics across many agents is actually needed.

Goal of this phase: prove the spine — a child writes a finding, ≥30 min later another child sees it as
injected "what's new" context without anyone repeating it, and you can talk to Mike and any child from
your phone while the server keeps running.

## Verified facts that shape this plan

- Server already has Claude CLI **v2.1.185** (> 2.1.181 floor), `tmux` 3.2a, `jq`, `flock`, `bq`, `git`.
  Outbound HTTPS works. Remote: `github.com/minhtrido2023/thanhdt.git`.
- `mike/` does **not** exist yet — greenfield under `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/`.
- **Linger is OFF** — must run `loginctl enable-linger trido` or systemd user units die on logout.
- **6 production cron jobs already exist** (papertrade 8:30, pt_8l 10:45, telegram 11:00, v34b refresh
  16:15, commodity 1st/10th). The new consolidator must be *added* and scheduled to avoid the 16:15
  BQ-heavy window. The `:07/:37` offset in the original plan is fine.
- Hooks injecting `additionalContext` have **no documented ~10k char cap** (original plan over-worried;
  keep `context_pack.md` lean anyway, for cost/latency, not because of a hard cap).
- Whether hooks fire for *subagents* is **uncertain** — but irrelevant here: every child is a
  **top-level remote-control session**, where hooks fire reliably. Do **not** make children Agent-tool
  subagents.

## Corrections folded in from the original plan

- **No `bq load` in this phase** → the duplicate-ingestion bug (re-loading full JSONL every 30 min) and
  the seconds-resolution `event_id` collision both disappear. When BigQuery is added later, load events
  into a staging table and `MERGE` on `event_id`, and generate `event_id` with a real UUID
  (`cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid`), not `(now|tostring)`.
- **Cron Tier-2 (autonomous `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions`) is deferred.** The 30-min cron in
  this phase is **mechanical only**: collect heartbeats → registry, rebuild `context_pack.md` from recent
  finding events, bump version, git commit. The *smart* digest/synthesis is done by **Mike interactively,
  on demand** when you ask — not by an unattended agent with write access to shared context.

## Directory layout (greenfield, under `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/`)

```
mike/
├── MIKE.md                      # Mike's operating handbook (imported into Mike's CLAUDE.md)
├── CHILD_TEMPLATE.md            # child handbook template
├── kb/
│   ├── KNOWLEDGE.md             # canonical KB (git; consolidator-written only)
│   ├── context_pack.md          # lean snapshot injected into children
│   └── version.txt              # monotonically increasing KB version
├── bus/
│   ├── inbox/<id>.jsonl         # each child appends its own events (append-only, one file per child)
│   ├── directives/<id>.jsonl    # Mike writes advisory directives for a child
│   └── registry/<id>.json       # heartbeat/status (child writes its own file atomically)
├── agents/<id>/                 # per-child git worktree (CLAUDE.md + .claude/settings.json)
├── locks/consolidator.lock
├── logs/{consolidator.log,watchdog.log}
├── bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}
└── hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}
```

- `stop.sh` — call `heartbeat.sh`, then inject the reminder to `append_event.sh` any durable knowledge
  produced this turn.

### 4. Mechanical consolidator (`bin/consolidate.sh` + `publish_context.sh`)
- `flock -n` on `locks/consolidator.lock` (single writer — never two).
- Read all `bus/registry/*.json` → mark any child with no heartbeat in >30 min as `dead` in a status
  view; collect recent `finding`/`answer`/`decision` events from `bus/inbox/*.jsonl`.
- `publish_context.sh` rebuilds `kb/context_pack.md` with a **"RECENT (since last version)"** section at
  the very top (this is what the hook injects as the delta), followed by stable pointers into
  `KNOWLEDGE.md`. Bump `kb/version.txt`. `git add -A && git commit`.
- **No `claude -p` in this phase.** Synthesis into `KNOWLEDGE.md` is done by Mike interactively.

### 5. Cron (add to existing crontab, do not replace the 6 jobs)
```
7,37 * * * * /home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/bin/consolidate.sh >> .../logs/consolidator.log 2>&1
```
Verify no overlap/contention with the 16:15 `daily_refresh_v34b_linux.sh`.

### 6. Sessions (systemd user units — durable, auto-restart)
- `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service` with `ExecStart=/usr/bin/claude remote-control --name "%i"`,
  `Restart=on-failure`, `RestartSec=20`, `WorkingDirectory=.../mike/agents/%i`.
- **Pick one model explicitly: one named remote-control session per child** (`mike@Mike`,
  `mike@Con-Research`, …). Drop the `--spawn=worktree --capacity` alternative for now — it's for ad-hoc
  client worktrees, not a persistent named fleet, and mixing the two only confuses operations.
- `spawn_child.sh <id> <role>`: create git worktree `agents/<id>`, render `CHILD_TEMPLATE.md` →
  `agents/<id>/CLAUDE.md` (imports `../../kb/context_pack.md`) + `.claude/settings.json` (the 3 hooks),
  write initial `registry/<id>.json`, then `systemctl --user enable --now mike@<id>`.

### 7. Watchdog (`bin/watchdog.sh`, small cron, e.g. every 10 min)
- Check `systemctl --user is-active mike@*`; restart any dead unit.
- Detect a dead bridge / likely-expired OAuth (e.g. unit flapping, or a probe that the remote-control
  session is offline) → fire a **PushNotification** so you know to re-auth. (Manual "reminder" from the
  original plan is too weak; make the failure *tell you*.)

## Critical files to create
- `mike/bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}`
- `mike/hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}`
- `mike/{MIKE.md, CHILD_TEMPLATE.md}`, `mike/kb/{KNOWLEDGE.md, context_pack.md, version.txt}`
- `~/.config/systemd/user/mike@.service`
- crontab entries (consolidator + watchdog)

Script *bodies* are in `MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md` §5–§7 and §10 — reuse them with the two corrections
above (UUID `event_id`; consolidator is mechanical-only this phase).

## Verification (acceptance for Phase-1)

1. `claude remote-control --name Mike` and one child both show online in the **mobile app**; you can chat
   with each directly. Kill your phone's network, reconnect → sessions still there (server kept running).
2. From the child session, produce a finding → `append_event.sh con-research finding "<topic>" '<json>'`
   writes to `bus/inbox/con-research.jsonl`.
3. Wait for the `:07/:37` cron (or run `consolidate.sh` by hand): `context_pack.md` gains a RECENT entry,
   `version.txt` increments, a git commit appears.
4. In a **second** child, submit any prompt → the `UserPromptSubmit` hook injects "KB updated → vN,
   recent changes: …" **without anyone repeating it**. This is the headline proof.
5. `reboot` the server (or `systemctl --user restart`) → units come back via linger + `Restart=`;
   registry shows heartbeats resume.
6. Stop a child for >30 min → consolidator marks it `dead` in the status view; watchdog/PushNotification
   behaves as configured.

## Explicitly deferred (Phase 2+, only if Phase-1 proves insufficient)
- BigQuery KB (4 `mike_*` tables, MERGE index) — add **with** staging-table + `MERGE`-on-UUID dedup.
- Autonomous Tier-2 `claude -p` digest/directive generation — keep strictly additive, bounded, audited;
  never let it rewrite `KNOWLEDGE.md`; consider hourly (not 30-min) cadence.
- Agent Teams live mailbox (experimental) — garnish only, never load-bearing.
- Monitor-based live reactions between 30-min ticks.

## Risks carried into this phase
- **OAuth expiry** kills the bridge + all children; fix is interactive re-login. Mitigation: watchdog →
  PushNotification, and know the token lifetime up front.
- **Network drop >~10 min** times out remote-control; `Restart=` brings it back, but the in-flight
  conversation is lost — all durable state must already be on the bus/git (it is, by design).
- **Companion-model limit (accepted):** Mike cannot make an idle child do background work unattended.
  Children act when you interact with them; directives are advisory, consumed on next interaction. If you
  later want true autonomous dispatch, that's the Hybrid model (per-child headless `claude -p` worker) —
  a Phase-3 decision, not this phase.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Mike Fleet — Phase-1 Spine (companion model, git-KB, no BigQuery)

## Context

You want a "Mike" orchestrator session on the **kaffa_v2** server that creates and coordinates child
Claude Code agents, all running as **remote-control** sessions so you can drive them from Claude Code
mobile/desktop without interruption, and have everything survive device disconnects. The full vision is
captured in `MIKE_ORCHESTRATOR_PLAN.md` (uploaded). That plan is technically sound — its capability
research was re-verified against current Claude Code behavior ([REDACTED]21) and holds up.

This plan file is the **reviewed, de-risked starting point**. Based on your three decisions:

1. **Companion model** — children are interactive remote-control sessions *you* drive. Mike routes
   questions and aggregates knowledge; it does **not** autonomously dispatch work into idle children.
   (This is the correct call: `send_message` [REDACTED] asks for confirmation, so remote-control alone
   gives Mike no unattended way to wake an idle child — the companion model sidesteps that entirely.)
2. **Phase-1 spine first** — file message-bus + mechanical cron. No BigQuery, no autonomous LLM Mike.
3. **Git-markdown KB first** — `KNOWLEDGE.md` + `context_pack.md` in git, JSONL bus. Add BigQuery later
   only if SQL history/analytics across many agents is actually needed.

Goal of this phase: prove the spine — a child writes a finding, ≥30 min later another child sees it as
injected "what's new" context without anyone repeating it, and you can talk to Mike and any child from
your phone while the server keeps running.

## Verified facts that shape this plan

- Server already has Claude CLI **v2.1.185** (> 2.1.181 floor), `tmux` 3.2a, `jq`, `flock`, `bq`, `git`.
  Outbound HTTPS works. Remote: `github.com/minhtrido2023/thanhdt.git`.
- `mike/` does **not** exist yet — greenfield under `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/`.
- **Linger is OFF** — must run `loginctl enable-linger trido` or systemd user units die on logout.
- **6 production cron jobs already exist** (papertrade 8:30, pt_8l 10:45, telegram 11:00, v34b refresh
  16:15, commodity 1st/10th). The new consolidator must be *added* and scheduled to avoid the 16:15
  BQ-heavy window. The `:07/:37` offset in the original plan is fine.
- Hooks injecting `additionalContext` have **no documented ~10k char cap** (original plan over-worried;
  keep `context_pack.md` lean anyway, for cost/latency, not because of a hard cap).
- Whether hooks fire for *subagents* is **uncertain** — but irrelevant here: every child is a
  **top-level remote-control session**, where hooks fire reliably. Do **not** make children Agent-tool
  subagents.

## Corrections folded in from the original plan

- **No `bq load` in this phase** → the duplicate-ingestion bug (re-loading full JSONL every 30 min) and
  the seconds-resolution `event_id` collision both disappear. When BigQuery is added later, load events
  into a staging table and `MERGE` on `event_id`, and generate `event_id` with a real UUID
  (`cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid`), not `(now|tostring)`.
- **Cron Tier-2 (autonomous `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions`) is deferred.** The 30-min cron in
  this phase is **mechanical only**: collect heartbeats → registry, rebuild `context_pack.md` from recent
  finding events, bump version, git commit. The *smart* digest/synthesis is done by **Mike interactively,
  on demand** when you ask — not by an unattended agent with write access to shared context.

## Directory layout (greenfield, under `/home/trido/thanhdt/WorkingClaude/mike/`)

```
mike/
├── MIKE.md                      # Mike's operating handbook (imported into Mike's CLAUDE.md)
├── CHILD_TEMPLATE.md            # child handbook template
├── kb/
│   ├── KNOWLEDGE.md             # canonical KB (git; consolidator-written only)
│   ├── context_pack.md          # lean snapshot injected into children
│   └── version.txt              # monotonically increasing KB version
├── bus/
│   ├── inbox/<id>.jsonl         # each child appends its own events (append-only, one file per child)
│   ├── directives/<id>.jsonl    # Mike writes advisory directives for a child
│   └── registry/<id>.json       # heartbeat/status (child writes its own file atomically)
├── agents/<id>/                 # per-child git worktree (CLAUDE.md + .claude/settings.json)
├── locks/consolidator.lock
├── logs/{consolidator.log,watchdog.log}
├── bin/{append_event.sh, heartbeat.sh, consolidate.sh, publish_context.sh, spawn_child.sh, watchdog.sh}
└── hooks/{session_start.sh, user_prompt_submit.sh, stop.sh}
```

## Build steps

### 1. Prereqs (one-time)
- `loginctl enable-linger trido` (so user services survive logout/reboot — currently OFF).
